reasoning: As of early November 2025, Bitcoin is trading around the $100,000 to $105,000 range. Throughout the year, the lowest recorded price was approximately $78,200 during a February 2025 market correction. Even during a significant flash crash on October 10, 2025, prices only fell to about $102,000–$104,000 across major exchanges. There is no evidence that the Binance 1-minute BTC/USDT candle has touched $50,000 or lower at any point since the market's start date of December 30, 2024. For the market to resolve "Yes", Bitcoin would need to experience a 50% price crash from current levels within the remaining ~50 days of the year, or suffer an extreme Binance-specific flash crash on its most liquid trading pair. While not entirely impossible due to Bitcoin's historical volatility and the nature of exchange anomalies, the time window is very short and a halving in price is highly improbable under normal market conditions at this stage. Therefore, the probability is estimated to be extremely low.
